title: Modal Verbs
description: Learn how to say 'I can', 'I must', and 'I want'. These verbs are small but super powerful!
explanation:
**HOOK**
Normal verbs are just actions (run, play, eat). But Modal Verbs are like seasonings—they change the 'flavor' of the whole sentence! They tell us if you are ABLE to do something, if you are FORCED to, or if you simply WANT to.

**CONCEPT**
We are learning: können (can), müssen (must), and wollen (want). When you use these, they always shove the second action verb all the way to the end of the sentence.

**RULE**
Modal Verb stays in Position 2 (the King). The Main Verb (what you are doing) stays untouched at the very end of the sentence.

tables.title: The Power 3 (Singular forms)
tables.note: TIP: In modal verbs, 'ich' and 'er/sie/es' are ALWAYS identical! No endings needed.

deep_notes.title: Satzklammer (Sentence Bracket)
deep_notes.text:
Think of the sentence as a bracket. The Modal Verb is the left side, and the Infinitive verb at the end is the right side. Everything else is caught in the middle! 
Example: Ich KANN morgen um acht Uhr KOMMEN. (I CAN COME tomorrow at 8).

examples.english: I can speak German. (Speak at the end!)
Do you want to eat a pizza?

exercises.question: Wo steht das zweite Verb bei einem Modalverb?
exercises.explanation: The second verb always stays at the very end of the sentence in its base form.

exercises.question: Sag 'I want to drink water':
exercises.hint: Subject + Modal + Object + Drink-verb at end.

exercises.question: Ein Gesetz (law) sagt: Du ____ hier nicht parken.
exercises.hint: It's a law! You ARE FORCED not to park here. (must)

exercises.question: Ich will essen. Er ____ auch essen.

exercises.instruction: Identify which modal verb was used in the sentence:
What is the meaning of the sentence you hear?
Identify the modal verb heard here:
exercises.explanation: wollen (willst) is for wishing or wanting.

exercises.instruction: Which action verb is at the end of the sentence?
exercises.explanation: Correct! 'warten' (to wait) is at the very end.
